message:
perf(pane_tiers): replace global RwLock<HashMap> with DashMap (ft-x2fvv)

The PaneTierClassifier sits on the per-byte hot path of every tracked
pane (`on_pane_output` is called from each pane's capture task), and
the previous `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>` serialized all of those
updates through one exclusive acquisition. On the 200+-pane fleet the
classifier was designed for this becomes the bottleneck — the scan_loop
thread holding the write lock fans out contention across every agent's
IO pump.

Swap to `dashmap::DashMap<u64, PaneState>` so each `register_pane`,
`unregister_pane`, `on_pane_output`, `set_background`, `set_rate_limited`,
`set_thinking`, and `classify` acquires only the write lock for the
single pane's shard, not a global exclusive lock. Contention factor
drops from N panes → ~N/shards; the rest of the public API is
unchanged (all methods still take `&self`, still return the same
types), so call sites throughout the runtime need no edits.

Add `dashmap = "6.1"` to `frankenterm-core`'s dependencies; Cargo.lock
picks up that addition plus the already-queued `criterion 0.5.1` and
`io-uring` deps for `frankenterm-mux-impl` that the lockfile had
drifted on (from commits 7adc68de and a52f5bbe in this branch).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
